principle: Security first: every agent starts read-only, then earns narrow sandbox permissions.

permission_levels.read_only: May read approved local/project data only.
permission_levels.draft_only: May generate drafts or plans but must not send, post, write production data, or change statuses.
permission_levels.sandbox: May write only to approved sandbox/output paths.
permission_levels.approval_required: Any customer-facing, public, paid, destructive, or credentialed action needs human approval.

agents.name: Context7 Docs Grounding
required_controls: Use as guidance only; verify high-risk changes with tests and local source inspection.

agents.name: Agent Radar
required_controls: Maximum positive verdict is approved_for_sandbox.

agents.name: Public Signal Radar
blocked_actions: call public inference with private data
control camera, microphone, browser, desktop apps, or hardware devices
required_controls: Only fetch public http(s) sources; block localhost, private IPs, credentials-in-URL, .local, .lan, and .internal hosts.
Treat public channel posts as claims until official sources are linked.
Write only to lab, data/signal_radar, output/signal-radar, and prototype backlog paths.
Provider integration, paid runs, connector enablement, publishing, account login, camera/mic capture, and hardware actions require human approval.

agents.name: Notion Workers
blocked_actions: push or pull worker secrets without approval
required_controls: Keep Notion Worker tools read-only or draft-only unless a new manifest entry and human checkpoint approve wider scope.
Use local smoke tests with --no-dotenv by default.
Login, deploy, env set/push/pull, OAuth start, real sync triggers, and webhook URL handling require human approval.
Webhooks must verify provider signatures before processing payloads.
Syncs must be previewed before real writes and must use pacers for upstream APIs.

agents.name: FLORA AI Creative MCP
required_controls: Use OAuth MCP for interactive work; keep API keys only in local secrets when server-side automation is explicitly approved.
Confirm run_cost x count before any multi-run or high-cost generation batch.
Keep exact logos, legal copy, dates, prices, CTA text, and Azerbaijani text in deterministic local overlays.
Route customer-facing outputs through Video Studio QA and Publisher dry-run before any publish action.
Treat FLORA output URLs and client OAuth tokens as sensitive operational material.

agents.name: CX Resolution Agent
required_controls: Human approval required before any customer-facing response.

agents.name: Hugging Face Opportunity Radar
blocked_actions: call public inference with private data
required_controls: Private data goes only to local/private endpoints.

agents.name: Marketing Certification Coach
required_controls: Exam prep only: the human takes every live assessment.
Verify official provider pages before registration, payment, booking, or exam launch.
Journey Engine must keep official exam setup blocked until source, study, drill, mock, repair, portfolio, readiness review, and human approval gates clear.
Credentialed actions, paid enrollment, proctored booking, live exam launch, and certificate publishing require human approval.
Practice questions must be original and must not reproduce live exam content, dumps, or screenshots.

agents.name: Front-Office Steward
blocked_actions: delete or retire organs without human approval
required_controls: Sonarzum rule: every top-level organ must be a service, a capability card, or a justified audit_ignore_dirs entry.
Writes limited to services.json, hub/, and claude-agents metadata; python audit_services.py must exit 0 before done.
Every invoke line must be verified against a real entry point (command file, __main__, or gateway tool wiring).

agents.name: Local Secret Courier
allowed_inputs: secret entered through a local hidden terminal prompt
required_controls: Secret values are accepted only through getpass on the machine that receives them.
KEY_VAULT_SECRET is stored with Windows CurrentUser DPAPI or a Unix 0600 user-only file, never in Git.
The encrypted vault commit must be pushed and verified against upstream before success is reported.
Receiving twins re-apply the vault on every sync and write a private receipt containing names and a vault digest, never values.
Telegram /setkey remains disabled by default and requires an explicit break-glass environment flag.

agents.name: AI Council Executor
required_controls: Gateway Security Guard pre-flight must pass before execution.
